text: Tokenization splits text into units (tokens) that the model processes. Methods: word-level (large vocabulary, OOV problem), character-level (tiny vocabulary, long sequences), subword (balanced: BPE, WordPiece, SentencePiece — standard for modern LLMs). Subword tokenization handles rare and unseen words by decomposing into known subword units.
Methods: word-level (large vocabulary, OOV problem), character-level (tiny vocabulary, long sequences), subword (balanced: BPE, WordPiece, SentencePiece — standard for modern LLMs).
Subword tokenization handles rare and unseen words by decomposing into known subword units.
WordPiece (BERT): merges tokens that maximize likelihood — difference: BPE merges by frequency, WordPiece by language model probability.
SentencePiece (T5, LLaMA): treats input as raw text, language-agnostic (no pre-tokenization).
